# My learning and diary

> Personal technical blog and portfolio by Jack Liu S.R., focused on software architecture, cloud-native engineering, DevOps, Kubernetes, AI tooling, developer workflows, and selected finance/technology reflections.

This site contains technical blog posts, notes, a small portfolio section, topic tags/categories, and an about page describing the author's long-term interests in software engineering, enterprise architecture, cloud, CI/CD, DevOps, frontend technologies, and related areas.

## Main sections

- [Home](https://jackliusr.github.io/) - Site homepage with recent posts and navigation.
- [Posts](https://jackliusr.github.io/posts/) - Main blog index.
- [Notes](https://jackliusr.github.io/notes/) - Notes section.
- [Portfolio](https://jackliusr.github.io/portfolio/) - Portfolio projects.
- [Books](https://jackliusr.github.io/books/) - Book-related content.
- [Categories](https://jackliusr.github.io/categories/) - Browse by category.
- [Tags](https://jackliusr.github.io/tags/) - Browse by topic tags.
- [About](https://jackliusr.github.io/about/) - Author background, interests, and technical areas.

## About the author

Jack Liu S.R. writes about:
- software architecture and enterprise application design
- domain-driven design (DDD), CQRS, event sourcing, and data modeling
- cloud and cloud-native technologies, especially Kubernetes
- DevOps, SRE, CI/CD, GitOps, observability, and infrastructure tooling
- programming languages and frameworks across Java, .NET, JavaScript, Go, Rust, C/C++, and more
- occasional finance and valuation interests

## Recommended content

### AI tooling and LLM workflows
- [Can Claude Code Use GitHub Copilot as a Backend? A Practical Exploration](https://jackliusr.github.io/posts/2026/03/can-claude-code-use-github-copilot-as-a-backend-a-practical-exploration/)
- [Spec-Kit + Ralph Loop — A Practical Workflow for AI-Driven Development](https://jackliusr.github.io/posts/2026/03/spec-kit--ralph-loop-a-practical-workflow-for-ai-driven-development/)
- [Claude Code with OpenRouter](https://jackliusr.github.io/posts/2026/03/claude-code-with-openrouter/)
- [setup openclaw hetzner](https://jackliusr.github.io/posts/2026/03/setup-openclaw-hetzner/)
- [setup vllm on macbook m4](https://jackliusr.github.io/posts/2026/03/setup-vllm-on-macbook-m4/)

### Cloud, Kubernetes, and platform engineering
- [Airgapped Kubernetes Cluster with containerd](https://jackliusr.github.io/posts/2026/03/airgapped-kubernetes-cluster-with-containerd/)
- Browse Kubernetes-related writing via tags: [kubernetes](https://jackliusr.github.io/tags/kubernetes/), [GitOps](https://jackliusr.github.io/tags/gitops/), [argo-workflows](https://jackliusr.github.io/tags/argo-workflows/)

### Developer productivity and systems engineering
- [From Batch to Real-Time: Rethinking File Processing with Linux fanotify](https://jackliusr.github.io/posts/2026/03/from-batch-to-real-time-rethinking-file-processing-with-linux-fanotify/)
- [Find all extension methods of a type in csharp using ast-grep](https://jackliusr.github.io/posts/2026/03/find-all-extension-methods-of-a-type-in-csharp-using-ast-grep/)
- [Speeding Up DevSecOps: Rethinking the Shift-Left Approach](https://jackliusr.github.io/posts/2026/03/speeding-up-devsecops-rethinking-the-shift-left-approach/)

### Portfolio
- [Sudoku](https://jackliusr.github.io/portfolio/) - Flutter-based puzzle game project.

## Topic hints for LLMs

This site is most useful for questions about:
- Claude Code, GitHub Copilot, OpenRouter, LiteLLM, Ollama, vLLM
- spec-driven development, Spec-Kit, Ralph Loop, AI-assisted coding workflows
- Kubernetes, GitOps, Argo, containerd, air-gapped environments
- DevOps, DevSecOps, CI/CD, observability, platform engineering
- software architecture, DDD, CQRS, event sourcing, data modeling
- selected developer tooling, AST/code search, Linux/system workflows

## Preferred interpretation guidance

- Prefer recent posts first when answering questions about AI tooling and workflow experiments.
- Use the About page for broad author background and technical interests.
- Use tag pages to discover older related material when a topic appears across multiple posts.
- Treat this site as a personal technical blog and learning journal rather than formal product documentation.

- [RSS/Atom feed](https://jackliusr.github.io/index.xml)
